articles.description: Digg laid off a significant number of staff and shut down its app, but says it's not giving up on the startup.

articles.title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
articles.description: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons.

articles.title: Facebook makes it easier for creators to report impersonators
articles.description: Facebook is making it easier for creators to report impersonators while tightening rules around reposts and other unoriginal content.

articles.title: Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
articles.description: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.

articles.title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
articles.description: The AI industry is constantly churning out news, like major acquisitions, indie developer successes, public outcry, and existentially dangerous contract negotiations.

articles.title: Travis Kalanick launches a new company called Atoms focused on robotics
articles.description: Kalanick's existing ghost kitchen company CloudKitchens will be rolled into Atoms, which also wants to get into mining and transport.

articles.title: The wild six weeks for NanoClaw’s creator that led to a deal with Docker
articles.description: Gavriel Cohen is living an open source developer's dream as his project has achieved acclaim and a partnership with Docker in a matter of weeks.

articles.title: Spotify will let you edit your Taste Profile to control your recommendations
articles.description: When you edit your Taste Profile, you'll impact your personalized playlists like Discover Weekly, recommendations, and Wrapped.

articles.title: The $32B acquisition that one VC is calling the ‘Deal of the Decade’
articles.description: According to Index Ventures Partner Shardul Shah, cybersecurity startup Wiz sits “at the center of three tailwinds: AI, cloud, and security spend.” Those tailwinds powered what just became the largest venture-backed acquisition in history — Google’s $32 billion deal, finalized after a declined

articles.title: Apple drops commission rates in China without a fuss
articles.description: Apple dropped its App Store commission rates to 25% in China, and commissions on auto-renewed subscriptions down to 12%.

articles.title: The FBI is investigating malware hidden inside games hosted on Steam
articles.description: The FBI believes a series of video games published on Steam in the last two years were embedded with malware by the same hacker.

articles.title: Peacock expands into AI-driven video, mobile-first live sports, and gaming
articles.description: Peacock is betting on new AI-powered video experiences, vertical clips, and mobile games to help its growth.

articles.title: Motional robotaxis join the Uber app in Vegas two years after major reset
articles.description: The launch comes just two years after a major reboot at Hyundai-owned Motional. The service will start with a safety monitor, which Motional hopes to remove by year's end.

articles.title: Before quantum computing arrives, this startup wants enterprises already running on it
articles.description: After selling his AI startup to AMD for $665 million, Peter Sarlin is back with Qutwo, a new venture building the infrastructure it believes enterprises will need when quantum computing finally arrives.

articles.title: Truecaller now lets you hang up on scammers — on behalf of your family
articles.description: Caller identity platform Truecaller recently launched a new feature that lets one person become an admin of a family group, get alerts about fraud calls received by other members, and even end a call on their behalf if they suspect a family member might get scammed.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
URL: https://claude.com/blog/1m-context-ga
Anthropic's million-token context window moves from beta to production.
This is the clearest signal yet that the context war is over and long-context won. Enterprise RAG architectures built around chunking and retrieval may face obsolescence risk faster than expected. Watch for pricing pressure on vector database vendors.

### Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock
URL: https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/qatar-helium-shutdown-puts-chip-supply-chain-on-a-two-week-clock
A single-point-of-failure in the semiconductor supply chain just got exposed.
Qatar supplies roughly 25% of global helium, critical for chip fab cooling and leak detection. Two weeks of buffer inventory means fabs could face production slowdowns by month-end. This is the kind of tail risk that doesn't show up in earnings models until it materializes.

### LLM inference infrastructure for a systems audience
URL: https://blog.mihirnanavati.com/
A systems-level breakdown of what it actually takes to serve LLMs at scale.
Useful technical primer for anyone trying to diligence AI infrastructure plays. The economics of inference—memory bandwidth, batching strategies, GPU utilization—remain the binding constraint on AI deployment margins.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### The $32B acquisition that one VC is calling the 'Deal of the Decade'
URL: https://techcrunch.com/video/the-32b-acquisition-that-one-vc-is-calling-the-deal-of-the-decade/
Google closes largest VC-backed acquisition in history with Wiz at $32B.
Index's Shardul Shah frames this at the intersection of AI, cloud, and security spend. The multiple signals Google's desperation to close the gap with AWS and Azure on cloud security posture. Secondary signal: public cloud security multiples just got repriced.

### Travis Kalanick launches a new company called Atoms focused on robotics
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/13/travis-kalanick-launches-a-new-company-called-atoms-focused-on-robotics/
Kalanick rolls CloudKitchens into a broader robotics play spanning kitchens, mining, and transport.
The ghost kitchen thesis is being quietly abandoned in favor of a physical automation rollup. Worth monitoring whether this becomes a real platform or a distressed asset aggregation strategy. Kalanick's operator playbook is aggressive deployment, which historically creates both opportunity and carnage.

### Elon Musk pushes out more xAI founders as AI coding effort falters
URL: https://www.ft.com/content/e5fbc6c2-d5a6-4b97-a105-6a96ea849de5
xAI's coding-focused AI effort is struggling, leading to founder departures.
The Grok coding assistant was supposed to be xAI's differentiator against Claude and GPT. Execution problems here suggest xAI's $18B valuation carries meaningful delivery risk. Talent churn at this stage is a red flag for any growth equity position.

### Apple drops commission rates in China without a fuss
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/13/apple-drops-commission-rates-in-china-25-percent/
Apple quietly cuts App Store take rate to 25% in China, 12% on subscriptions.
Regulatory pressure works. This is a meaningful margin compression for Services, Apple's highest-margin segment. Expect this to become the template for other jurisdictions. The 30% rate is now indefensible as a global standard.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### The wild six weeks for NanoClaw's creator that led to a deal with Docker
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/13/the-wild-six-weeks-for-nanoclaws-creator-that-led-to-a-deal-with-docker/
Solo developer goes from open source project to Docker partnership in six weeks.
A case study in how developer tools distribution has fundamentally changed. Docker's acqui-hire velocity suggests they're building something significant around containerized dev environments. Open source remains the most efficient GTM for infrastructure tools.

### I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites
URL: https://benzimmermann.dev/blog/algolia-docsearch-admin-keys
Security researcher finds widespread credential exposure in documentation tooling.
Algolia's DocSearch product is embedded across thousands of developer sites. Admin key exposure means write access to search indices—potential for SEO poisoning or data exfiltration. This is a reminder that SaaS sprawl creates attack surface that doesn't show up on security audits.

### Show HN: Context Gateway – Compress agent context before it hits the LLM
URL: https://github.com/Compresr-ai/Context-Gateway
Open source tool addresses the cost problem of long-context LLM calls.
Even with 1M context windows available, the economics of stuffing them full are brutal. Context compression middleware is an emerging category. Watch for inference cost optimization to become its own market layer.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The helium story and Wiz acquisition capture the week's real tension: physical supply chains remain fragile while software valuations continue to reprice around AI and security. Anthropic shipping 1M context to GA while xAI struggles with coding suggests the frontier model race is stratifying faster than expected—execution discipline matters more than compute access at this stage. The Apple China commission cut is the slow erosion story that matters most for large-cap tech margins over the next 24 months.
